title: Add knowledge-card production-depth guard tests
description: Add focused tests that distinguish seeded contract-proof cards from production-depth Ekologus knowledge. The current tests prove shape/basic blockers, but not whether source lineage/freshness/coverage are deep enough for daily content usefulness.
design: Guard source_lineage, freshness, service-specific coverage and missing-card blockers. Do not require arbitrary card counts unless tied to reviewed source pack. Prefer tests that prevent internal-only seeded cards from being described as production-depth.
acceptance_criteria: Tests fail if a production-depth readiness label can be returned from internal-only seeded cards, if broad environmental terms overmatch unrelated topics, or if missing service-specific CTA/claim/evidence cards do not block Sales Brief/draft readiness.
close_reason: Knowledge-card production-depth guards implemented: content knowledge responses expose production_depth_readiness, seeded cards return seeded_contract_proof/ready_for_daily_content=false, source-backed cards remain review-required, broad environmental terms no longer unlock service-card matching, and focused Python/shared/dashboard tests passed.

title: Expand typed Ekologus knowledge cards from reviewed sources

Keep broad guardrail cards, but do not let them masquerade as production-depth service knowledge.
design: Implement in typed WILQ content knowledge modules with source_lineage, confidence and freshness. No broad RAG/vector DB. No prompt-only behavior. Cards must remain evidence companions, not evidence replacements.
acceptance_criteria: Knowledge card inventory includes reviewed service-specific cards and tests prove required service/CTA/claim/evidence cards block Sales Brief/draft when missing. Cards with only internal seeded lineage cannot unlock production-depth status.

title: Harden measurement outcome provenance for content decisions
description: Second-opinion follow-up after Goal 005 activation: verify and harden whether content measurement outcome interpretation is formally tied to metric_store facts, connector refresh/JobRun provenance, evidence IDs and the original content work item decision. Request typing for content POSTs is already fixed, so this task is about measurement provenance and false-success prevention, not Zod request schemas.
design: Keep outcome interpretation conservative. Do not create fake attribution science or SEO scores. Measurement hardening should make provenance explicit: which metric facts, connectors, refresh/job lineage, dates and work_item_id support an outcome. If live WILQ API is unreachable, work from local contracts/tests and record live-proof blocker.
acceptance_criteria: Audit or implementation proves one of: (a) outcome interpretation is tied to stored metric facts/job or connector refresh provenance with tests, or (b) a precise blocker states what metric_store/JobRun linkage is missing. Tests must prove no success/failure claim without metric evidence IDs, allowed metric names, source connectors, matching work_item_id/window and measurement readiness.
close_reason: Implemented fail-closed measurement outcome provenance with metric_fact_ids, refresh_run_ids, matching work item/window/url, connector and allowed metric checks plus focused tests.

title: Define evidence-based draft variant selection guard
description: Goal 004 can produce draft variants, but Wilku still needs to understand which variant is safest/useful and why. Define a bounded selection/recommendation guard so variant choice is based on evidence coverage, service fit, buyer problem, CTA fit, duplicate risk and quality review findings, not the prettiest text.
design: Do not create a fake SEO score or new generation flow. API should expose variant comparison as blockers/dimensions/safe next step. React only renders the API-owned comparison.
acceptance_criteria: A design or implementation task exists for variant comparison with no magic score, no publish-ready claim, and tests planned/proven for generic-SEO variant blocking and evidence-bound recommended variant selection.
close_reason: Implemented API-owned draft variant selection guard with evidence dimensions and focused tests

title: Audit Sales Brief v2 signal quality
description: Assess whether enrichment inputs for Sales Brief v2 are dense, fresh and useful enough for real Ekologus content decisions. Separate connector freshness issues from model/card interpretation issues.
design: No fake SEO scores. Use WILQ evidence/source connector metadata, GSC/GA4/Ahrefs/WordPress signal density, service fit, buyer problem, CTA hypothesis and measurement baseline. Weak evidence must become a typed blocker or low-confidence state, not a polished generic brief.
acceptance_criteria: Audit identifies for current queue candidates whether signal quality is sufficient, thin, stale or blocked; names the weak connector/domain cause; and defines exact API/test changes needed before claiming daily content usefulness.
close_reason: Completed Sales Brief v2 signal quality audit, fixed enrichment source-fact lineage and filed follow-ups for missing knowledge cards/blocked snapshot

title: Design read-only Ekologus Service Profile review surface

design: API-owned Service Profile view model. Dashboard renders Polish operator language. Any future write/update to knowledge cards requires human review/audit. No skill-only or React-only knowledge behavior.
acceptance_criteria: A concrete API/dashboard design is ready for a read-only Service Profile with card status, source lineage, freshness, coverage gaps and flag-for-review semantics. It explicitly blocks direct knowledge writes and says what tests will prove it.
close_reason: Read-only Service Profile review surface designed in docs/architecture/service-profile-review-surface.md. title: Goal 004 wilq content operator skill and UAT harness
description: Create a WILQ content operator skill over WILQ API and a Wilku UAT harness for real content work sessions. The skill orchestrates queue, enrichment, draft variants, quality, revision, human review, draft-only handoff and measurement without becoming the writer.
design: Use skill-creator rules when implementing the skill. The skill consumes WILQ API only, refuses unsafe shortcuts, does not call OpenAI or WordPress directly, and must pass adversarial evals.
acceptance_criteria: Skill smoke/evals prove no direct content writing, no dev canonical, no publish, no skipped gates, no success claim before measurement and correct Polish operator output; UAT packet covers 3-5 real Ekologus work items.
close_reason: Implemented WILQ content operator skill and UAT harness over WILQ API; smoke proves queue/enrichment/knowledge/WP draft-only/measurement gates.

title: Goal 004 anti-slop execution guard
description: Keep Goal 004 from adding broad cleanup, monolith growth, prompt-only behavior, fake scores, stale aliases, broad RAG or React product logic while adding the operations layer.
design: Use changed-file budgets, targeted tests, contract inventory and frozen-growth checks. Add retrieval only after typed cards/rules/validators/evals are solid.
acceptance_criteria: Every Goal 004 slice records focused verification, no new feature growth in frozen monoliths, no deprecated alias retention when direct migration is feasible, and full scripts/verify.sh passes before completion.
close_reason: Goal 004 anti-slop guard satisfied: every slice recorded focused verification, frozen monolith growth stayed clean, no prompt-only/RAG/React product-brain behavior was added, and full rtk scripts/verify.sh passed before Goal 004 closure.

title: Goal 004 measurement outcome interpreter
description: Add conservative measurement outcome interpretation after measurement windows: not ready, insufficient data, noisy/inconclusive, directional improvement, likely underperformance, or measured success.
design: No success/failure before earliest verdict date. No causality theater. Outcome interpretation must feed future queue/knowledge decisions only with source evidence and confidence.
acceptance_criteria: Tests prove early windows block outcome claims, missing data returns insufficient/noisy states, and measured outcomes require source evidence, observation period and explicit limitations.
close_reason: Implemented conservative measurement outcome interpreter with typed measurement-outcome API, not_ready/insufficient/noisy/directional/underperformance/success states, early-claim blocking, evidence requirements and no causality theater. Proof: targeted measurement pytest subset, shared schema Vitest, Ruff, source mypy, Fallow audit, changed-file complexity audit and git diff --check.

title: Goal 004 draft variants through structured outputs
description: Add claim-gated draft variants for refresh, service-led, problem-led and FAQ/supporting content paths using WILQ API + OpenAI SDK Structured Outputs only after gates.
design: Variants are typed artifacts with evidence maps, blocked claims and publish_ready=false. No mass generation and no direct Codex writing.
acceptance_criteria: Fake SDK and API tests prove variants require gates, keep publish_ready=false, cite evidence/source connectors, reject forbidden claims and never write to WordPress.
close_reason: Implemented claim-gated draft variants via WILQ API and Structured Outputs contracts. Variants cover preserve-first refresh, problem-led, service-led and FAQ/supporting paths, keep publish_ready=false and wordpress_write_allowed=false, and pass fake SDK runtime proof plus focused tests.

title: Goal 004 WordPress draft-only adapter boundary
description: Harden the WordPress handoff path for optional real draft-only creation while keeping dry-run default and structurally blocking publish/destructive update.
design: No publish. No destructive update. Adapter must prove post_status=draft, publish_allowed=false, destructive_update_allowed=false, audit envelope present and external writes explicit.
acceptance_criteria: Tests prove dry-run default, blocked live without explicit config/review/audit, draft-only creation shape when enabled, no publish path and no destructive updates.
close_reason: Implemented explicit WordPress draft-only execution boundary with typed allowed operation, dry-run default, live adapter readiness, structural publish/destructive blocks, API blocked-live default and domain fake-adapter draft-only proof. Proof: targeted WordPress/content pytest subset, shared schema Vitest, dashboard API/route Vitest, dashboard typecheck, Ruff, source mypy, Fallow audit, changed-file complexity audit and git diff --check.

title: Goal 004 operations-grade Sales Brief from enrichment and knowledge
description: Upgrade the sales brief so it consumes ContentOpportunityEnrichment, Ekologus knowledge cards, service fit, buyer problem, CTA guidance, claim policy and measurement boundary.
design: Brief is a typed API contract, not a prompt. No service fit/card/evidence means no draft.
acceptance_criteria: Tests prove the operations-grade Sales Brief blocks missing service fit, missing required cards, missing evidence, unresolved claims and absent measurement plan; brief fields are exposed through API and shared schemas.
close_reason: Implemented operations-grade Sales Brief v2 consuming ContentOpportunityEnrichment and typed knowledge cards. Brief now exposes operations context, knowledge constraints and measurement boundary fields, and blocks missing/blocked enrichment. Proof: focused pytest subset, dashboard Vitest subset, typecheck, Ruff, mypy, complexity audit, Fallow audit and git diff --check passed.

title: Goal 004 bounded revision application
description: Turn revision plans into versioned, bounded draft changes with diff, audit trail and mandatory quality-review rerun.

acceptance_criteria: Tests prove revisions are versioned, diffed, evidence-bound, re-reviewed, blocked on hard findings, and cannot affect another work_item_id.
close_reason: Implemented bounded revision application with revision-apply API, versioned diff entries, mandatory updated quality-review rerun, wrong-work-item blocking and focused tests. acceptance_criteria: API tests prove revision instructions are concrete, evidence-bound and cannot bypass claim/preflight/measurement blockers.
close_reason: Implemented bounded revision plan API backed by ContentQualityReview findings

description: Expose Content Measurement Window v1 through the focused content workflow API so a content work item can schedule the post-handoff observation window without allowing early success/failure claims.
design: Extend wilq/content/workflow/api.py and apps/api/wilq_api/routers/content_workflow.py. Reuse wilq.content.measurement.window build/apply/outcome helpers. Do not add measurement logic to frozen monoliths, do not claim outcomes before earliest_verdict_date, and keep React out of product decisions.
acceptance_criteria: Focused API tests prove POST /api/content/work-items/measurement-window returns a planned window for a valid item/handoff, applies measurement_window_status/id to the returned work item, blocks missing metrics, blocks missing source connectors, blocks dev preview canonical, keeps success_claim_allowed=false while planned, and exposes outcome blockers before earliest_verdict_date. Ruff, mypy, audit_complexity --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check pass.
close_reason: Completed Measurement Window API bridge: POST /api/content/work-items/measurement-window calls the typed Content Measurement Window v1 builder, returns a planned window or blockers, applies measurement_window_status/id only when created, keeps success_claim_allowed=false while planned, exposes early outcome blockers, and blocks missing metrics, missing connectors and dev canonical. Focused API/domain tests, Ruff, mypy, audit_complexity --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check passed.

title: Goal 002 expose content WordPress draft handoff API
description: Expose WordPress Draft Handoff v1 through WILQ API after Draft Package and approved Human Review. This should prepare a typed draft-only handoff contract without writing to WordPress or publishing.
design: Extend the focused content workflow API module/router. The endpoint should accept ContentWorkItem, Draft Package, Human Review and WordPress draft audit envelope, call build_content_wordpress_draft_handoff, and return typed handoff/blockers. Do not call WordPress connector, do not create a post, do not publish and do not allow destructive updates.
acceptance_criteria: Focused API tests prove POST /api/content/work-items/wordpress-draft-handoff returns a prepared draft-only handoff for valid item/review/audit, blocks missing audit, blocks non-approved review, blocks dev preview canonical, keeps publish_allowed=false and destructive_update_allowed=false, and preserves evidence IDs. Ruff, mypy, audit_complexity --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check pass.
close_reason: Completed WordPress Draft Handoff API bridge: POST /api/content/work-items/wordpress-draft-handoff calls the typed prepare-only builder, returns prepared draft-only handoff or blockers, and never calls WordPress or publishes. Focused API tests prove valid prepared handoff, missing audit blocker, non-approved review blocker, dev canonical blocker, publish_allowed=false, destructive_update_allowed=false and evidence preservation; Ruff, mypy, audit_complexity --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check passed.

title: Goal 002 expose content human review API
description: Expose Human Review v1 through WILQ API so a Draft Package can be approved or blocked by a typed human decision before any WordPress draft handoff.
design: Extend the focused content workflow API module/router. The endpoint should accept ContentWorkItem, ContentHumanReview, optional Draft Package and optional Claim Ledger, call content_human_review_blockers and apply_content_human_review_to_work_item, and return typed blockers, reviewed item state and whether WordPress handoff is allowed. Do not create WordPress handoff or write to WordPress.
acceptance_criteria: Focused API tests prove POST /api/content/work-items/human-review updates the work item for approved draft review, blocks missing reviewer/checklist/evidence, blocks needs_changes from WordPress handoff, requires blocked claims to be handled, and returns no WordPress handoff. Ruff, mypy, audit_complexity --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check pass.
close_reason: Completed Human Review API bridge: POST /api/content/work-items/human-review calls typed Human Review v1 blockers, updates ContentWorkItem only after blocker-free review, reports wordpress_handoff_allowed without creating handoffs. Focused API tests prove approved review state update, missing reviewer/checklist/evidence blockers, needs_changes blocker and blocked-claim handling; Ruff, mypy, audit_complexity --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check passed.

title: Goal 002 expose content draft package API
description: Expose Draft Package v1 through WILQ API after ContentWorkItem preflight and Sales Brief. This moves content workflow from API-visible brief to API-visible outline-first draft package without WordPress handoff or publication.
design: Extend the focused content workflow API module/router. The endpoint should accept ContentWorkItem, inventory records/duplicate risk, Claim Ledger, Sales Brief seed facts and optional supplied Sales Brief. It should compute inventory resolution and ContentPreflight v2, build or use the Sales Brief, then call build_content_draft_package. Return typed preflight, sales brief result and draft package result. Do not generate full prose with an LLM, do not call Codex, do not create WordPress drafts, and keep publish_ready=false.
acceptance_criteria: Focused API tests prove POST /api/content/work-items/draft-package returns an outline-first not-publish-ready Draft Package for a valid draft_allowed item, blocks when preflight is not draft_allowed, blocks when Claim Ledger has unresolved/measurement-blocked claims, preserves section-to-evidence mapping, and returns no WordPress handoff. Ruff, mypy, audit_complexity --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check pass.
close_reason: Completed Draft Package API bridge: POST /api/content/work-items/draft-package computes inventory resolution and ContentPreflight v2, builds or accepts Sales Brief v1, then calls the typed Draft Package v1 builder. Focused API tests prove outline-first publish_ready=false package creation, preflight-not-draft-allowed blocking, Claim Ledger blocking, no WordPress handoff and section-to-evidence mapping; Ruff, mypy, audit_complexity --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check passed.

title: Goal 002 expose content sales brief API
description: Expose the existing Sales Brief v1 domain contract through WILQ API after ContentWorkItem preflight. This moves the content workflow from API-visible preflight to API-visible brief generation without adding prompt-only drafting or React-side product logic.
design: Extend the focused content workflow API module/router. The endpoint should accept ContentWorkItem, inventory records/duplicate risk, Claim Ledger and Sales Brief seed facts, compute inventory resolution and ContentPreflight v2, then call build_content_sales_brief. It must return typed preflight and Sales Brief build result, preserving evidence IDs/source connectors and blocking brief creation when preflight or final canonical/measurement/source facts are invalid.
acceptance_criteria: Focused API tests prove POST /api/content/work-items/sales-brief returns a typed brief for a valid preserve-first item with evidence-backed source facts, blocks dev preview canonical or missing source facts, preserves forbidden claims from claim ledger, and does not create draft/WordPress artifacts. Ruff, mypy, audit_complexity --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check pass.
close_reason: Completed Sales Brief API bridge: POST /api/content/work-items/sales-brief computes Content Inventory resolution and ContentPreflight v2, then calls the typed Sales Brief v1 builder; focused API tests prove valid preserve-first brief generation, missing source-fact blockers, forbidden claim visibility, and no draft/WordPress artifacts; Ruff, mypy, audit_complexity --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check passed.

title: Goal 002 expose content work item preflight API
description: Expose the existing ContentWorkItem, Content Inventory v1 and ContentPreflight v2 domain contracts through a WILQ API endpoint without adding product logic to React, skills or frozen monolith files. This is the first API bridge from domain-only content workflow contracts to operator/runtime usage.
design: Create a focused router/module for content workflow API contracts. The endpoint should accept a typed ContentWorkItem plus inventory records/duplicate risk, call wilq.content.inventory.records.resolve_content_inventory and wilq.content.preflight.workflow.build_content_preflight_verdict, and return typed data with evidence/source connectors preserved. Do not add draft generation, WordPress writes, prompt logic, deprecated aliases or dev URL canonical exceptions.
acceptance_criteria: A focused API contract test proves POST /api/content/work-items/preflight returns blocked for dev/preview canonical, plan_allowed or brief_allowed for a valid preserve-first item, evidence IDs and source connectors are preserved, and no existing /api/content/preflight response shape changes. Ruff, mypy, audit_complexity --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check pass.
close_reason: Completed first content workflow API bridge: POST /api/content/work-items/preflight exposes ContentWorkItem, Content Inventory v1 and ContentPreflight v2 through a focused router/domain API model; focused API tests prove dev preview canonical is blocked, valid preserve-first item preserves evidence/source connectors and existing GET /api/content/preflight shape stays unchanged; Ruff, mypy, audit_complexity --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check passed.

title: Goal 002 Content Measurement Window v1
description: Add a post-publication measurement window model for content work items so WILQ cannot claim success or failure before evidence exists.
design: Measurement window records baseline period, observation period, earliest verdict date, allowed metrics, source connectors and current status. No success/failure language before the window is ready for review.
acceptance_criteria: Tests prove content outcome claims are blocked before the measurement window closes and that every WordPress draft handoff schedules or requires a measurement window.
close_reason: Completed Content Measurement Window v1 baseline: added measurement window contract with baseline period, observation period, earliest verdict date, allowed metrics, source connectors, evidence IDs and optional WordPress handoff link. Outcome claims stay blocked before the window reaches ready_for_review/closed after earliest_verdict_date; handoff requires or schedules a measurement window. Checks passed: tests/content/test_measurement_window.py, tests/content, Ruff on touched files, mypy on the new measurement module, import-boundary smoke, scripts/audit_complexity.py --changed --allow-frozen and staged git diff --check.

title: Goal 002 WordPress Draft Handoff v1
description: Implement a gated WordPress draft handoff for approved content packages only, with audit and no publication.
design: The handoff creates or prepares a WordPress draft only after approved human review. No automatic publish, no destructive update of existing canonical content and no dev URL canonical target.
acceptance_criteria: Tests prove no WordPress draft handoff without human review, audit envelope, final canonical URL and safe draft status. Publish/apply remains blocked.
close_reason: Completed WordPress Draft Handoff v1 baseline: added prepare-only draft handoff contract requiring public final canonical URL, matching Draft Package, approved Human Review and audit envelope with evidence IDs. Handoff blocks dev preview canonical targets, non-approved review, missing audit, mismatched draft packages and publish-ready draft packages; it emits post_status=draft, publish_allowed=false and destructive_update_allowed=false, and only marks workflow draft_created when a real WordPress post ID is provided. Checks passed: tests/content/test_wordpress_handoff.py, tests/content, Ruff on touched files, mypy on new handoff/workflow modules, import-boundary smoke, scripts/audit_complexity.py --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check.

title: Goal 002 Human Review v1
description: Add explicit human review records for content brief, claim ledger, draft package and WordPress draft handoff decisions.
design: Robot cannot mark content publish-ready. Review events must record reviewer, decision, notes, checked items, evidence IDs and blocked claims handled.
acceptance_criteria: Tests prove WordPress draft handoff is blocked without approved human review, and review results are available to the content workflow state.
close_reason: Completed Human Review v1 baseline: added typed content human review records for sales brief, claim ledger, draft package and WordPress handoff stages. Review requires reviewer, decision, checked items, evidence IDs and handled blocked claims; only approved review can update ContentWorkItem to human_review_status=approved and unblock the WordPress handoff gate. Checks passed: tests/content/test_human_review.py, tests/content, Ruff on touched files, mypy on new review/workflow modules, import-boundary smoke, scripts/audit_complexity.py --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check.

title: Goal 002 Draft Package v1
description: Create a typed draft package workflow that can produce outline-first content only after preflight, sales brief and claim ledger gates pass.
design: Draft packages are not publish-ready. They must include brief_id, claim_ledger_id, section_to_evidence_map, claims_used, claims_removed_or_blocked, human_review_questions and publish_ready=false.
acceptance_criteria: Tests prove drafts are blocked without preflight, sales brief and claim ledger, and generated draft packages always include evidence mapping and human review questions.
close_reason: Completed Draft Package v1 baseline: outline-first draft packages now build only after draft_allowed preflight, matching Sales Brief, matching Claim Ledger and source-fact evidence mapping. Packages include brief_id, claim_ledger_id, section-to-evidence map, publish-ready claims, blocked claims removed, human review questions and publish_ready=false. Checks passed: tests/content/test_draft_package.py, tests/content, Ruff on touched files, mypy on the new module, import-boundary smoke, scripts/audit_complexity.py --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check.

title: Goal 002 Claim Ledger v1
description: Add a structured claim ledger for content promises, SEO/performance claims, compliance/environmental-adjacent claims and business outcome claims.
design: Claims are data, not prompt instructions. Statuses include allowed_with_evidence, allowed_general, needs_human_review, blocked and blocked_until_measurement. Unsupported promises of rankings, leads, revenue, full legal compliance or guaranteed outcomes are blocked.
acceptance_criteria: Tests prove blocked claims cannot appear as publish-ready language and draft generation remains blocked when required claim review is missing.
notes: Slice proof 2026-06-30: added Claim Ledger v1 in wilq/content/claims/ledger.py. Claims are structured entries with type, status, evidence IDs, reason and optional reviewer. Deterministic rules block guarantee claims, block SEO/performance/business outcome claims until measurement is ready, require human review for legal/risk/environmental claims and block allowed_with_evidence entries that have no evidence. Focused tests prove blocked claims are excluded from publish-ready claims and draft remains blocked when required claim review or evidence is missing. Checks passed: tests/content/test_claim_ledger.py, tests/content (67 tests), Ruff on touched files, mypy on the new module, import-boundary smoke for wilq/content, scripts/audit_complexity.py --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed Claim Ledger v1 baseline: claims are structured data with allowed, review-required, blocked and blocked-until-measurement statuses; blocked or unreviewed risky claims cannot be publish-ready and draft remains blocked when claim review/evidence is missing.

title: Goal 002 Sales Brief v1
description: Create an API-owned sales/content brief contract for Ekologus content work items before any draft is generated.
design: The brief is not a prompt. It must include buyer problem, buyer trigger, target reader, search intent, service fit, source facts, evidence IDs, final canonical target, existing content plan, H1/H2/FAQ/CTA direction, internal links, forbidden claims, missing evidence and measurement plan.
acceptance_criteria: A sales brief endpoint/service can build a structured brief only from a valid work item/preflight. Tests prove no brief is produced without required evidence and final URL semantics.
notes: Slice proof 2026-06-30: added Sales Brief v1 in wilq/content/briefs/sales.py. The brief is a typed contract built only from ContentWorkItem, ContentPreflight v2, Content Inventory v1, Claim Ledger v1 and an explicit seed with buyer problem, buyer trigger, target reader, search intent, service fit, source facts, H1/H2/FAQ/CTA direction, internal links, missing evidence and measurement plan. Focused tests prove no brief is produced without required evidence/source facts, valid final canonical semantics, preflight allowance or measurement plan, and that dev preview URLs are rejected as final canonical. Checks passed: tests/content/test_sales_brief.py, tests/content (74 tests), Ruff on touched files, mypy on the new module, import-boundary smoke for wilq/content, scripts/audit_complexity.py --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed Sales Brief v1 baseline: typed brief contract now builds only from valid work item/preflight/inventory/claim ledger inputs, with required evidence, final canonical URL, source facts, forbidden claims and measurement plan. No draft generation or React-side decision logic was added.

title: Goal 002 ContentPreflight v2
description: Turn the current 'Czy można pisać?' surface into a first-class API workflow verdict with blocked, plan_allowed, brief_allowed, draft_allowed and handoff_allowed states.
design: Preflight cannot draft or write. Draft is allowed only after sales brief and claim ledger. Handoff is allowed only after human review. React must render API-owned labels and disabled reasons.
acceptance_criteria: Focused tests cover no evidence, missing connector, missing final canonical, dev URL as canonical, existing content preserve-first, duplicate risk, missing brief and missing human review.
notes: Slice proof 2026-06-30: added ContentPreflight v2 workflow verdict in wilq/content/preflight/workflow.py. The verdict exposes blocked, plan_allowed, brief_allowed, draft_allowed and handoff_allowed states over ContentWorkItem plus Content Inventory v1. Preflight still does not draft or write: it only exposes create_allowed, sales_brief_allowed, draft_allowed and wordpress_draft_allowed booleans with blockers. Focused tests cover no evidence, missing connector, missing final canonical, dev preview as canonical, existing content preserve-first, duplicate risk, missing brief, missing human review and full handoff allowed. Checks passed: tests/content/test_preflight_workflow.py, tests/content (61 tests), Ruff on touched files, mypy on the new module, import-boundary smoke for wilq/content, scripts/audit_complexity.py --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed ContentPreflight v2 domain verdict: blocked, plan_allowed, brief_allowed, draft_allowed and handoff_allowed states now compose ContentWorkItem and Content Inventory v1 without drafting, writing or React-side decision logic.

title: Goal 002 Content Inventory v1
description: Build a first content inventory layer that can answer what content exists on Ekologus public sources, what final canonical URL is known, and whether a topic should preserve, refresh, merge, create after review or block.
design: Inventory is a domain service, not React logic and not skill logic. Dev preview URLs are not historical SEO evidence and must not become final canonical URLs.
acceptance_criteria: Content inventory returns structured records with URL/canonical/status/source/evidence fields and focused tests cover existing URL, missing final canonical, dev preview as non-canonical and duplicate-risk inputs.
notes: Slice proof 2026-06-30: added Content Inventory v1 records/resolution in wilq/content/inventory/records.py. Inventory now returns structured records and a resolution with URL, final canonical, preview, source connectors, evidence IDs, blockers and preserve/create/block mode. Focused tests cover existing public URL preserve-first, missing final canonical blocker, dev preview URL as invalid canonical, unresolved duplicate risk blocking create, clear duplicate check allowing create_after_review and canonical deduplication. Checks passed: tests/content/test_inventory_records.py, tests/content (52 tests), Ruff on touched files, mypy on the new module, import-boundary smoke for wilq/content, scripts/audit_complexity.py --changed --allow-frozen and git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed Content Inventory v1 baseline: structured inventory records and resolution now support preserve-first decisions, missing canonical blockers, dev-preview canonical rejection, duplicate-risk blockers and create_after_review only after a clear duplicate check.
